interpretation: FAM174A encodes a membrane protein that may play a role in cellular signaling and interactions. However, the retrieved evidence does not directly link FAM174A to the mechanisms of Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH). The associations found with other conditions, such as neuroendocrine neoplasms and diabetes mellitus, suggest potential indirect pathways but lack specificity to SONFH. Therefore, the evidence is weak, and the interpretation relies heavily on inference regarding its biological relevance to SONFH. As such, FAM174A is unlikely to serve as a clinically actionable biomarker for early detection of SONFH.
relevance_summary: FAM174A does not have a strong connection to Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH) based on current evidence.

result.title: FAM174A ↔ neuroendocrine neoplasm
result.text:
Disease: neuroendocrine neoplasm
Description: Endocrine tumors, also referred to as neuroendocrine tumors (NETs), are defined by a common phenotype which is characterized by the expression of general markers (neuron specific enolase, chromogranin, synaptophysin) and hormone secretion products. These tumors may be localized in any part of the body and are generally discovered in non-specific situations, i.e. not immediately suggestive of NETs (tests for inherited predisposition to tumors or for a clinical syndrome caused by abnormal hormone secretion).
Association score: 0.198

result.title: FAM174A ↔ type 2 diabetes mellitus
result.text:
Disease: type 2 diabetes mellitus
Description: A type of diabetes mellitus that is characterized by insulin resistance or desensitization and increased blood glucose levels. This is a chronic disease that can develop gradually over the life of a patient and can be linked to both environmental factors and heredity.
Association score: 0.029
